methods.description: Process for preparing halite samples and analyzing fluid inclusions to determine homogenization temperature (Th).

steps.description: Halite samples were collected from two depths (3028.04 m and 3188.61 m).
steps.searchable_summary: collect halite samples from specified depths

steps.description: Halite samples were chosen by XRD (D/max-rA12kw, Rigaku Corporation, Japan).
Split the halite samples with a hammer and chisel along cleavage planes, into fragments with thicknesses of 0.5 to 1 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: split halite samples into thin fragments

steps.description: Observe and photograph these cleavage flakes under the microscope, and document the occurrence and morphology of primary fluid inclusions.
steps.searchable_summary: microscopically analyze and document fluid inclusions

steps.description: Seal halite samples in self-sealing plastic bags, put into an airtight plastic box with desiccant, and transfer into a Haier freezer for about 1 week.
steps.searchable_summary: store halite samples in controlled environment

steps.description: After the single-phase fluid inclusions were frozen to nucleate bubbles, measure Th using a Linkam THMSG600 heating and cooling stage.
steps.searchable_summary: measure homogenization temperature using heating and cooling stage

steps.description: The heating rate of the stage was first set at 0.5uC/min, but was lowered to 0.1-0.2uC/min when approaching 20uC.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust heating rate during Th measurement
